license_or_terms_summary: The Odds API terms (reviewed 2026-06-11) permit using odds data in analytical tools, dashboards, and user-facing applications, including commercial use, provided the data is not resold, repackaged, or redistributed as a standalone data product (no raw feeds, downloadable files, or derivative data services). Provider names may be referenced factually without implying endorsement.
derived_storage: data/processed/odds_2026.parquet (gitignored, regenerated from code); only de-margined benchmark probabilities and aggregate comparisons appear in committed reports and notebooks
decision: The Odds API v4 is confirmed as the World Cup 2026 odds provider. The authenticated capability probe on 2026-06-11 succeeded with the personal credential loaded from the ignored local .env: the sports endpoint exposed exactly one active men's FIFA World Cup soccer competition (sport_key soccer_fifa_world_cup, title 'FIFA World Cup', non-outright), and its odds endpoint returned 71 events, all 71 carrying an exact home/draw/away three-outcome h2h market in decimal format. Terms permit analytical benchmark use without redistributing raw payloads, therefore raw provider responses are stored only under the gitignored data/raw/odds/ path. The authored CSV template data/external/odds_2026_template.csv remains the manual import fallback if provider coverage lapses during the tournament.

probe_evidence.method: Authenticated GET on /v4/sports and /v4/sports/soccer_fifa_world_cup/odds (markets=h2h, regions=eu, decimal); credential supplied only via the ODDS_API_KEY environment variable and redacted from all logs and artifacts
